unit.name: RNN architecture, strengths, and weaknesses
unit.description: Presents the recurrent neural network as a shared-parameter sequence model and explains both its advantages over fixed windows and its practical limitations.
unit.summary: An RNN is introduced as a family of networks that reuse one set of weights across time steps, updating a hidden state as each token is read [ts=3471s] [ts=3516s] [ts=3600s]. The hidden state starts from an initial value, combines the previous state with the current word embedding, adds a bias, applies a nonlinearity such as tanh, and the final hidden state is fed through a softmax to predict the next word [ts=3616s] [ts=3646s] [ts=3691s] [ts=3779s]. This gives variable-length context with fixed model size and shared processing, but it remains sequentially slow and simple RNNs tend to let distant information fade too quickly [ts=3816s] [ts=3850s] [ts=3898s] [ts=3977s].

key_points.text: RNNs reuse the same weights across successive time steps in a sequence.
The hidden state is updated from the previous hidden state and the current word embedding at each step.
Bias terms and a nonlinearity, commonly tanh, are part of the recurrent update.
The final hidden state is passed through a softmax to produce a next-word distribution.
RNNs handle arbitrary context length without increasing model size, because context is summarized in a fixed-size hidden vector.
Recurrent computation is slow because it is inherently sequential.
Simple RNNs forget information from far back in the sequence too quickly.

unit_summary_for_generation: An RNN is introduced as a family of networks that reuse one set of weights across time steps, updating a hidden state as each token is read [ts=3471s] [ts=3516s] [ts=3600s]. The hidden state starts from an initial value, combines the previous state with the current word embedding, adds a bias, applies a nonlinearity such as tanh, and the final hidden state is fed through a softmax to predict the next word [ts=3616s] [ts=3646s] [ts=3691s] [ts=3779s]. This gives variable-length context with fixed model size and shared processing, but it remains sequentially slow and simple RNNs tend to let distant information fade too quickly [ts=3816s] [ts=3850s] [ts=3898s] [ts=3977s].

kp_catalog.name: Recurrent neural networks
kp_catalog.description: Shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context. Updating a hidden state from current input and previous hidden state at every time step.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because recurrent neural networks formalizes how shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Autoregressive language modeling
kp_catalog.description: Training a model to predict the next token from its prefix, used directly for decoder pretraining and as a baseline pretraining objective more broadly. Autoregressive models make sequence modeling tractable by factoring p(x) into conditional next-part distributions over an ordered decomposition of x.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because autoregressive language modeling formalizes how training a model to predict the next token from its prefix, used directly for decoder pretraining and as a baseline pretraining objective more broadly, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.
